page: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The server includes 1843 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and processing capabilities. The server achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 15933.0 and an image processing score of 15819.0, both categorized in the strong tier. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 43.73 GB/sec. For language model tasks, the system demonstrates strong text generation performance with a small 135M model at 296.4 tokens/sec, while larger models like the 70B variant show weaker text generation at 2.74 tokens/sec. Standard CPU and memory benchmarks, such as the Passmark CPU score of 41779.09 and Redis SET operations at 45.93 million operations per second, fall within the average performance tier.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial 1843 GB local storage, the DC24ds_v3 is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and web serving. The hardware profile offers a balanced qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise applications requiring dedicated Intel Xeon processing power and large memory capacities without the overhead of GPU accelerators. It is well-suited for relational databases, software compilation, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure DC24ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes 1843 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers strong performance in multi-core processing, static web serving, and image processing, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 15933.0 and web throughput of 43.73 GB/sec. It is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise web serving, and software compilation workloads.
